Greene County Recreation
Greene County is home to many parks, pools and recreation facilities, all offering residents and visitors numerous spots to spend their leisure time.  Recreation sites include three pools: Greene County Waterpark and Tennis Courts, Carmichaels Pool, Mon View Pool and Roller Rink.
Pools are seasonal and are open Memorial Day through Labor Day excluding days when school is in session. The Waterpark opens at noon, and Carmichaels and Mon View open at 1 p.m.  All pools close at 7 p.m. and are subject to inclement weather and staff availability.  Swim lessons are available at $25 per child/week.

Regional and Local Plan Released for Comment
The Southwest Corner WDB, in collaboration with our regional partners, is pleased to present WIOA Multi-Year Regional and Local Plans (PY2025 - PY2029), which have been posted for a public comment period from May 7, 2025 to June 9, 2025.

Public Notice Greene County Business Incubator Operations and Strategic Plan

Our Vision and Mission for Greene County
Vision
The Greene County Commissioners envision a vibrant and resilient future where every resident thrives, where equity and opportunity are paramount, and where collaboration drives progress. Guided by innovation and compassion, we aspire to lead with integrity, transparency, and dedication, building strong partnerships with community stakeholders, fostering sustainable development, and ensuring the well-being of all. Together, we strive to create an interconnected community– one county, under God– where residents flourish and achieve their full potential.
Mission
The
mission
of the commissioners is to represent and serve the residents of Greene County with honesty, integrity, and compassion while upholding the standards of fiscal responsibility, transparency, equity, and sustainability. Placing our faith in God as our cornerstone, we strive to enhance the quality of life for all residents by delivering essential services and fostering economic development. We commit to fostering intentional communication with community stakeholders and diversifying industries through creative development as a dedicated and collaborative team. By delivering exceptional, responsive public services and initiating both small- and large-scale transformational projects, the commissioners aim to foster growth and prosperity in our community while preserving the unique character of our county.

The County holds a wide variety of public records but there is no central repository. These records are held by the office responsible for them. Many of these records are available online for a fee and all are available to the public from within each office. The above links list most commonly requested public records but please call the
Commissioners Office
if you do not see what you need. Greene County complies with the Pennsylvania Right-to-know law and that process is described
here
. Many current records are on the website as well.

Assessment Office
You can pay your property taxes online or at the office of the Treasurer. If your taxes are delinquent, payments can be made at the Tax Claim Bureau. Both offices are on the first floor of the Greene County Office Building located at 93 East High Street in Waynesburg, PA 15370.
Your Property Taxes are based on the assessed value of your property and the millage rate set by the taxing authority; which can be your local municipality, your school disctrict, or the county. Assessment information is available in the Assessment office on the second floor of the Greene County Office Building located at 93 East High Street in Waynesburg, PA 15370.

Information Technology
Greene County offers a variety of GIS and mapping products. The department that can assist you depends on the type of mapping information you are requesting.  Parcel and Plat maps are available from the Assessment Office. Maps with respect to lot splits and land development are available through the Planning Commission. Addressing information is available through the Emergancy Management Agency. The GIS Technician in the Planning Commission can provide GIS data for specific queries and large datasets. The County also has full aerial photography of the entire county, provided by
Eagleview/Pictometry
and sales of imagery can be obtained from the Planning Commission. The County provides GIS data in ESRI shapefiles and MapInfo Tab files.
